world.title: Microsoft Restores Copilot Ribbon Option in Office
world.description: Windows Central, WindowsLatest, and coverage aggregated by Ghacks report that Microsoft is rolling back the default floating **Copilot** action button in **Word** and **Excel**, and adding an option to move the assistant back to the ribbon. The change follows user backlash that the floating button obstructed workspace, especially in **Excel**, and could not be easily dismissed, according to WindowsLatest and Ghacks. Ghacks and WindowsLatest report the update will begin rolling out in late May 2026. Windows Central cites a Microsoft statement that begins, "We've been working to make Microsoft 365 feel more connected and integrated with Copilot... We're listening, learning, and improving as we go," and notes the company acknowledged the floating placement harmed workflows even as engagement rose.
